title: Buying Chickens
description: Buying chickens from John the farmer.
story: I got to drive a pickup truck for the first time! I drove around thirty minutes down the highway to the Common Grounds farm and John and a couple of other people working there met me. I held the cage open while they caught the chickens from their coop and put them in. I exchanged cash and fresh cow milk for the chickens.

title: Separating Baby Goats
story: We had to catch and separate the baby goats at night so that we could milk their moms in the morning. Some of the goats were really hard to catch, especially this pair of twins names Nutmeg and Peanut. Sometimes just catching those two would take more than half the time. But the baby goats were so cute.

title: Milking Cows
story: We milked the cows every morning with a small machine and then strained and chilled the milk. Sometimes the cows were really not cooperative and it would take a while to get them in the milking stanchion, but even once in the stanchion they sometimes were still not having it. One cow once ran and dragged the whole milking stanchion after her it was crazy.

title: Collected Eggs
description: Every day before noon we would collect and count the chicken eggs.
story: I would collect all the chicken eggs in the morning and separate the easter eggs(the turqioseish eggs) so that we could incubate them. I started out when the chickens had just been moved to a new coop so I got to see the whole progression of only getting three eggs each day to around twelve.

title: Separating Lambs
description: We separated the lambs every morning.
story: I used to have to chase and catch the lambs every morning, but after two weeks I had socialized them enough to the point where they would come running to me every morning. They're the cutest ever and they had such distinct personalities -- I miss them so much.

title: Black Soldier Flies
description: Took care of black soldier fly larvae.
story: I would take care of the black soldier fly larvae which are a great source of nutrients for chickens but also compost and are great for sustainable farming. There's actually a bit of a crazy story about how I acquired the larvae, but it was really fun and I got to meet a cool dude.

title: Emma the Cat
story: Every day I would pet Emma because she was the cutest cat there. Emma, however, was the biggest food thief and everyone would literally throw her over the fence sometimes and she would come running right back to steal our food.

title: Chicken Coop
description: Cleaning and composting the chicken coop.
story: I had to turn and fluff and add new organic material to the chicken coop almost everyday to keep it clean and fresh

title: Cheese
story: Sometimes we made cheese with the milk from the farm and it tasted great but there was a lot of washing to do after.
